pattern: Counting non-decreasing sequences over a small fixed alphabet is a running-total dynamic program over which symbol each string currently ends on, which also collapses to a closed-form combinations count.
hints: Think about what it means for a string over just five symbols to never decrease -- once you move to a later symbol in the sequence you can never use an earlier one again.

Track, for each of the five symbols, how many valid strings of the current length end exactly on that symbol; a string of length L ending on symbol i extends any string of length L-1 that ended on symbol i or earlier.

Rolling that running total forward one length at a time turns the whole count into a handful of cumulative sums over five buckets -- or, recognized differently, into a single combinations formula that skips the simulation entirely.

edge_inputs.description: small length beyond the statement examples

stress.description: random lengths across the whole allowed range, biased toward the maximum

complexity.time: O(1) for the combinations form (O(n) for the dp oracle)
